slides.title: Places and Community
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 3 English, Unit 4: Places and Community. Welcome to Unit 4! In this unit you will explore the places in your community and the people who help you there. You will learn to name places like the hospital, the market, the court, the college and the garden, and to talk about helpers like the doctor, the officer and the sailor. You will describe places using words like quiet and busy, give clear instructions, ask questions, and explain your ideas with the word “because”. By the end, you will be ready to write your own report about a place in your community — just like Amal does in this unit's story. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name places in your community and the people who work there
Describe a place using adjectives and opposites, like quiet and busy
Give simple instructions using imperatives (“Exit the court quietly.”)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name places in your community and the people who work there. you will also describe a place using adjectives and opposites, like quiet and busy. you will also give simple instructions using imperatives (“exit the court quietly.”). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: doctor, officer, sailor, hospital, court, college. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Our Community and A Poem: “Places I Know”. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Imperatives (Giving Instructions)
bullets: An imperative is a special way to use a verb to give a command or an instruction. It tells someone to do something. We need imperatives to give clear...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Imperatives (Giving Instructions). An imperative is a special way to use a verb to give a command or an instruction. It tells someone to do something. We need imperatives to give clear directions, like “Exit the court quietly” or “Go to the hospital if you are sick.” Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 — My Community. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
